The player moved DOWN. The white block is now at row 27, and the green base is at rows 24-26. The player is now "above" the left target structure (id=5 at rows 28-31).
Interestingly, the left target area now has TWO structures:
1. id=5: dark gray frame at rows 28-31 with blue center at rows 29-30
2. id=9: charcoal frame at rows 32-35 with blue center at rows 33-34
It seems like the left target "duplicated" or "split" into two parts. The player is now between the green base (rows 24-26) and the target (rows 28-31), with the white block at row 27.
Let me try SPACE again to see if it collects the target now that the player is closer.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"Reward: {last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
